title: I Cannot Be Measured
description: Shimamura worries she's gained weight, but there's no scale in their apartment. Adachi's solution? A bridal carry—warm, earnest, and utterly perfect.
content:
After we moved in together, there was one thing I hadn't paid attention to at all—until it suddenly struck me one afternoon. Muttering to myself, 'It's not here… it's really not here,' I checked the bathroom, then the kitchen, searching every drawer and corner. Only after turning half the apartment upside down did I finally accept the truth: There's no scale here.

'Shimamura?'

Adachi looked over to me from the kitchen, an apple in one hand and a peeler in the other, her head tilted in mild confusion. I was crouched on the floor like some suspicious forager.

'There's no scale in this house,' I announced.

'Oh… right. I don't think we ever listed 'scale' on the moving checklist.'

Her tone had a clear 'Is that supposed to be a problem?' kind of innocence. Maybe even back at her parents' home, she'd never cared about things like that. Adachi hardly eats, and she's not the type to fuss about calories or portions.

But me? Even back then, as a fairly ordinary high school girl, I did think about my weight every now and then.

'It's just… I feel like my face has gotten rounder lately. Maybe I'm imagining things, but…'

Earlier, while washing my face, I caught myself staring at my reflection a little too long. Something felt off. I prodded my cheek—never a good idea—and got the uneasy sense that things were starting to shift in a… less-than-ideal direction.

'I think you're just imagining it.'

Adachi continued peeling the apple, the steady sound of the blade moving along the skin filling the room. Then she began cutting the slices neatly.

'I look at your face every day. I'd know.'

She says things like that so naturally now—plain, earnest, and a little embarrassing. It's adorable. But at the same time, it makes me realize how much she's grown.

She really has become more like an adult.

'…Hearing you say that does make it feel like it was all in my head.'

'Either way, the apple's ready. Here, come eat.'

'And just like that, my burning resolve to diet crumbles…'

Still, I took the slice she offered and bit into it. The crisp texture and soft sweetness spread pleasantly through my mouth.

'You look really cute holding an apple in both hands.'

Since we didn't bother with plates, I was just eating straight from my hands—and I didn't expect Adachi to compliment me for something so simple.

Cute? Me? Honestly, I probably looked more like a kid who'd just been handed a treat after club activities.

'Mmf… I sh'd… m've…'

'What? I didn't catch that.'

The apple stuffed in my cheek made my words completely unintelligible. I hurried to chew and swallow before trying again.

'I said I should probably start exercising.'

'Ohh, so that's what that was.'

I walk between home and the station every day for work, but once I'm at the office I'm glued to my desk. I can't exactly claim to be getting much physical activity.

'Exercise, huh… how about taking more walks?'

On our days off, whenever Adachi and I go shopping, we always end up wandering around the streets together. Usually it's just me pointing at something and saying, 'Hey, this looks nice,' and Adachi agreeing softly, 'Yeah.' With just that simple rhythm, we end up strolling for ages.

Something so simple, so effortlessly ordinary—and yet it fills me with warmth every time. Happiness that asks for nothing more than these quiet moments: tiny steps taken together, side by side, unhurried and enough.

'Or maybe some strength training? I heard you can use the community center's gym if you pay a small fee.'

The apple was delicious. After I'd finished eating it, I washed my hands and sat back down, squeezing my upper arm. Hmm… still couldn't tell whether I'd actually gained weight or not.

'Shimamura, are you trying to lose weight?'

'I'm still in the middle of investigating whether I've gotten heavier.'

'Then… then I'll be your scale.'

'Adachi?'

'Stand up for a second.'

Adachi rose to her feet first, rolling her shoulders and bending her knees a little as if warming up. Curious, I stood as well.

And the next moment—'Wah!'

A reflexive squeak escaped me.

Adachi lifted me. A full-on bridal carry—straight out of a storybook.

Our eyes met as she held me up, and we both burst into small, startled smiles. She spun once, lightly, almost playfully, before setting me gently back onto the floor.

She exhaled softly, then announced in a strangely solemn tone—as if delivering medical results:

'No change from before.'

'Aha… haha.' I couldn't help laughing.

What kind of scale even is this? Cute, warm, and way too earnest. With someone like her, why would I need anything else? Besides, it's not like Adachi would ever let anyone else do something like this anyway.

'Careful you don't hurt your back,' I said.

'Mhm.'

She patted her lower back to be sure, then twisted from side to side like she was doing a cooldown. Even after lifting me, she still remembered to take care of herself.

'…Wait. Am I heavy?'

'No no no, not at all!'

Adachi's quick, frantic denial made it obvious she was trying to hide something—but that flustered panic of hers scattered bright, sparkling colors around us. Like confetti in the air.

'Ahahaha!'

I burst out laughing again.

For a moment, time felt perfectly still. A happiness so full it almost hurt in my chest.
